meta.title: Thao Maha Brahma (Erawan Shrine) — Guide to Bangkok's Four-Faced Brahma Shrine
meta.description: Guide to Thao Maha Brahma (Erawan Shrine / 四面佛), the four-faced Brahma shrine at Ratchadamri, Bangkok — history, legends, worship customs, getting there and visiting hours

hero.subtitle: Thao Maha Brahma (Erawan Shrine / 四面佛) is a four-faced Brahma shrine at the Ratchadamri intersection — a center of faith and belief that has stood for over 60 years in the heart of Bangkok
hero.plannerNote: Updated with live weather at Thao Maha Brahma (Erawan Shrine)
hero.lightCaption: Times calculated from the shrine's actual coordinates (13.7443°N, 100.5397°E)

about.label: About
about.heading: What is Thao Maha Brahma (Erawan Shrine)?
about.lead: A four-faced Brahma shrine in central Bangkok believed to answer prayers for career, love, health and study
about.paragraph1: Thao Maha Brahma (Erawan Shrine) stands at the corner of Ratchadamri and Phloen Chit roads, Pathum Wan, opposite CentralWorld and the Grand Hyatt Erawan. It was built in 1956 during the construction of the Erawan Hotel to dispel misfortunes that struck the project.
about.paragraph2: The Brahma image is plaster over a wooden core, gilded in gold. The four faces represent creation, prosperity, protection and benevolence. Today it is one of Thailand's most famous sacred sites, free to visit daily 06:00–22:00.
about.quote: Built as an act of faith, it has stood for over 60 years in the heart of the capital

highlights.title: Four faces
highlights.desc: Each face brings a different blessing; devotees pray at each face in turn

highlights.title: Dance troupes
highlights.desc: When a wish comes true, devotees hire traditional dance troupes as thanks

highlights.title: City center
highlights.desc: At Ratchadamri intersection, easily reached by BTS Chit Lom and surrounding malls

editorTip.text: Come at dawn or in the evening to avoid heat and crowds. 18:00–20:00 has beautiful light and several dance troupes performing together

rituals.label: Worship
rituals.heading: Worship customs & offerings
rituals.quote: With a focused mind and sincere faith, what you truly wish for shall come to pass
rituals.intro: Worship at Thao Maha Brahma follows long-standing traditions — the offerings, the clockwise walk and the dance troupes all carry meaning. Here is a basic guide for first-time visitors.

steps.title: Light 3 incense, joss powder, 1 pair of candles
steps.desc: Buy offerings at the stall, light 3 incense sticks with joss powder and candles, and calm your mind

steps.title: Walk clockwise around the shrine
steps.desc: Circle the shrine to the right (clockwise) and pause to pray at each face respectfully

steps.title: Pray at each face
steps.desc: Each face represents a different blessing; make a separate wish at each one

steps.title: Hire a dance troupe (when granted)
steps.desc: When a wish is fulfilled, devotees hire a dance troupe to perform as an act of gratitude

offerings.title: Flowers (jasmine, ylang, rose)
offerings.desc: Fragrant flowers are the basic sacred offering, often made into garlands

offerings.title: Incense, candles & joss powder
offerings.desc: 3 incense sticks and a candle pair are used for prayer; joss powder is sprinkled

offerings.title: Fruit & ceremonial items
offerings.desc: Seasonal fruit and other items are placed on the offering table

offerings.title: Red cloth & donations
offerings.desc: Red cloth and donations are commonly offered for good fortune

rituals.dance: Dance troupes are hired to perform for the deity. When a wish is granted, devotees return to hire a troupe in gratitude. Some troupes have dozens of performers and several perform together in the evening, creating a beautiful and festive atmosphere.
tips: Dress modestly and remove hats while praying
Walk clockwise; never go against the direction
Avoid photographing people mid-prayer without permission
Silence your phone and keep quiet
No eating or smoking within the shrine area

history.label: History
history.heading: History & legends of Thao Maha Brahma (Erawan Shrine)
history.intro: From Hindu deity worship absorbed into Thai Brahmin rituals, this shrine grew from a small corner into a symbol of Bangkok

blocks.title: Origins (1956)
blocks.text: During construction of the Erawan Hotel several fatal accidents occurred. A Brahmin priest was consulted and a Brahma shrine was built in front of the hotel for good fortune; the misfortunes then ceased.

blocks.title: The four-faced belief
blocks.text: The four faces represent creation, long life, prosperity and protection. Worshippers walk clockwise and pray at each face in turn.

blocks.title: The 1994 incident
blocks.text: A mentally ill man smashed the image with a hammer; an angry crowd killed him. The original was preserved in a museum and a replica was made.

blocks.title: The current image
blocks.text: After a 2006 attack, a new bronze-and-gold image replaced the plaster one for durability, and it remains open for worship to this day.

legend.title: The legend of its sacredness
legend.text: It is said that those who pray with sincere faith and have their wishes granted often return to give thanks with dance troupes. This belief made the shrine one of Asia's most famous wish-making sites, especially among visitors from China, Hong Kong, Singapore and Taiwan.

didYouKnow.title: Did you know?
didYouKnow.text: Although Brahma is a Hindu deity, the shrine is maintained by Thai Brahmin ritual and is revered equally by Buddhists and Hindus.

folklore.title: Tales from the elders
folklore.tale: It is said Brahma is the creator god, so people offer flowers, scents and dance to show gratitude.
folklore.shrine: When life succeeds, they speak of the merit made at this shrine, and return to pray again and again.

guide.label: Visit Guide
guide.heading: Plan your visit & info
guide.lead: Essential information for planning your trip, safety and getting there and back

twoZone.title: Two ways to visit
twoZone.intro: Choose what suits you — a casual stop or a dedicated prayer visit

items.name: Casual visit
items.desc: View the image, take photos and buy flowers to offer on the spot, no preparation needed

items.name: Devoted prayer
items.desc: Prepare flowers, incense, candles and joss powder; walk clockwise and pray at each face

scamTip.text: Some people approach offering 'lucky strings' or press for donations. Buy offerings only from the stall in front of the shrine and never share personal details.

items.title: Entry
items.desc: Free — no entry fee

items.title: Offerings at the stall
items.desc: Flowers, incense, candles from ~20 baht

items.title: Dance troupe
items.desc: Hireable from ~2,000 baht up

items.title: BTS Chit Lom
items.desc: Exit 1, ~5 min walk to the shrine

items.title: MRT subway
items.desc: Lumphini station, then bus or taxi

items.title: Car / taxi
items.desc: Set destination to Erawan Shrine near CentralWorld

items.title: From Suvarnabhumi Airport (BKK)
items.desc: Take the Airport Rail Link (ARL) to Phaya Thai station, transfer to the BTS Sukhumvit Line, and alight at Chit Lom station (Exit 1).

items.title: From Don Mueang Airport (DMK)
items.desc: Take the A1 bus to BTS Mo Chit, transfer to the BTS Sukhumvit Line, and alight at Chit Lom station; or take a taxi via tollway.

photography.title: Photography
photography.intro: Tips for beautiful shots of the image and dance troupes

times.desc: Soft light, few people, calm atmosphere
Best golden light for the image and dance
Pretty lights, good for long exposure

spots.name: In front of shrine
spots.view: Clear view of the gilded image

spots.name: CentralWorld upper level
spots.view: Wide view of shrine and intersection

tips: Turn off flash to avoid disturbing prayers
Respect the sacred area; don't step on the shrine base

bestTime.title: Best time to visit
bestTime.intro: Open daily, but times of day differ greatly in weather and atmosphere

seasons.name: Quietest
seasons.desc: Cooler and uncrowded, ideal for peaceful prayer

seasons.name: Liveliest
seasons.desc: Beautiful light and several dance troupes performing together

seasons.name: Hot & crowded
seasons.desc: Very hot with heavy crowds and little shade

items.title: Watch your bag
items.desc: Crowded area has pickpockets; keep valuables secure

items.title: Dress modestly
items.desc: Not as strict as a temple, but dress respectfully at a religious site

items.title: Cross carefully
items.desc: The shrine is at a major intersection; use safe crossings

items.title: Beat the heat
items.desc: Midday is very hot; rest in nearby mall shade

hazeSeason.text: Early in the year Bangkok may face PM2.5 from burning and traffic. Those sensitive to dust should wear a mask and check the AQI before leaving.

emergency.title: Emergency numbers
emergency.intro: Keep these numbers handy for emergencies

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.intro: The shrine is at street level and easy to access

items.title: Ramped pavements
items.desc: Ramps and even ground; wheelchairs can access

items.title: Parking
items.desc: Park at CentralWorld, Gaysorn Village or nearby hotels

items.title: Restrooms
items.desc: Use restrooms inside the nearby shopping malls

items.title: Elderly friendly
items.desc: Open space with seating and shade from surrounding trees

faqs.a: Entry is free, but offerings and dance troupes cost as chosen
Open daily 06:00–22:00, good for both morning stops and evening prayer
Each face is believed to answer career, love, health and study; pray separately at each
BTS Chit Lom Exit 1 is a ~5 min walk, or take a car/taxi to CentralWorld
Not as strict as temples, but dress modestly and remove hats while praying

items.name: CentralWorld
items.desc: Major mall with many restaurants and shops

items.name: Gaysorn Village
items.desc: Upscale community mall next door, walkable

items.name: Big C Ratchadamri
items.desc: Supermarket and budget eateries

items.name: Ratchaprasong
items.desc: BTS Chit Lom–Phloen Chit junction with surrounding shops

food.label: Food around the shrine
food.heading: Restaurants & snacks near Thao Maha Brahma
food.lead: Surrounded by malls and eateries at every level, from cheap snacks to fine dining

dishes.name: Upper-floor food courts
dishes.desc: CentralWorld and Gaysorn have affordable, tasty food courts

dishes.name: Street food on Ratchadamri
dishes.desc: Curry, noodle and fresh-fruit stalls open all day

dishes.name: Cafés & bakeries
dishes.desc: Cafés in Gaysorn are good for a cool break

dishes.name: Hotel buffets
dishes.desc: Grand Hyatt Erawan offers international buffets

quote.text: Don't eat in front of the image and respect those praying — that is the visitor's graceful etiquette
quote.author: Guide editor

featured.text: I come every year at Chinese New Year; the atmosphere is sacred and the dance troupes are always beautiful
非常灵验的许愿地，四面分别代表不同愿望，绕圈祈福很有仪式感

items.text: First visit; impressed by the gilded image, staff were kind
items.name: Anong · Chiang Mai

items.text: A must-visit spiritual spot in Bangkok. The dance troupes are mesmerizing.
items.name: James Carter · London

items.text: Crowded in the evening but the light is gorgeous for photos
items.name: Piyawat · Bangkok

items.text: 許願很靈，還願時請了舞蹈團，現場氣氛莊嚴又熱鬧
items.name: Mei Chen · Taipei

items.text: Free and easy to reach, but very hot at midday
items.name: Somchai · Nakhon Pathom

items.text: Conveniently located next to CentralWorld, easy to combine with shopping.

items.text: 夜のライトアップが美しく、踊りの奉納も見応えがあった
